task.title: Configuring the rate limiting of packets by using the tc-ctinfo utility

li.p: The iperf3 utility is installed on a server and a client.

context.p: With rate limiting, you can limit network traffic, prevent the exhaustion of resources in the network, and reduce the load on servers by limiting repetitive packet requests in a specific time frame.

The connection tracking entry stores the Netfilter mark and connection information. When a router forwards a packet from the firewall, the router either removes or modifies the connection tracking entry from the packet. The connection tracking information (ctinfo) module retrieves data from connection tracking marks into various fields. This kernel module preserves the Netfilter mark by copying it into a socket buffer (skb) mark metadata field.

step.cmd: Perform the following steps on the server:

li.p: Add a virtual link to the network interface:
li.codeblock: # ip link add name ifb4eth0 numtxqueues 48 numrxqueues 48 type ifb
li.p: This command has the following parameters:

dd.p: Sets a new virtual device interface.
Sets the number of transmit queues.
Sets the number of receive queues.
Sets the type of the new device.

li.p: Change the state of the interface:
li.codeblock: # ip link set dev ifb4eth0 up

li.p: Add the qdisc attribute on the physical network interface and apply it to the incoming traffic:
li.codeblock: # tc qdisc add dev enp1s0 handle ffff: ingress
li.p: In the handle ffff: option, the handle parameter assigns the major number ffff: as a default value to a classful qdisc on the enp1s0 physical network interface, where qdisc is a queueing discipline parameter to analyze traffic control.
Add a filter on the physical interface of the ip protocol to classify packets:
li.codeblock: # tc filter add dev enp1s0 parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match u32 0 0 action ctinfo cpmark 100 action mirred egress redirect dev ifb4eth0
li.p: This command has the following attributes:

dd.p: Sets major number ffff: for the parent qdisc.
Sets the u32 filter to match the IP headers the of u32 pattern. The first 0 represents the second byte of IP header while the other 0 is for the mask match telling the filter which bits to match.
Sets action to retrieve data from the connection tracking mark into various fields.
Copies the connection tracking mark (connmark) 100 into the packet IP header field.

dt.codeph: action mirred egress redirect dev ifb4eth0

dd.p: Sets the action to mirred to redirect the received packets to the ifb4eth0 destination interface.

li.p: Add a classful qdisc to the interface:
li.codeblock: # tc qdisc add dev ifb4eth0 root handle 1: htb default 1000
li.p: This command sets the major number 1 to root qdisc and uses the htb hierarchy token bucket with classful qdisc of minor-id 1000.
Limit the traffic on the interface to 1 Mbit/s with an upper limit of 2 Mbit/s:
li.codeblock: # tc class add dev ifb4eth0 parent 1:1 classid 1:100 htb ceil 2mbit rate 1mbit prio 100
li.p: This command has the following parameters:

dd.p: Sets parent with classid as 1 and root as 1.
Sets classid as 1:100 where 1 is the number of parent qdisc and 100 is the number of classes of the parent qdisc.
The htb classful qdisc allows upper limit bandwidth of 2 Mbit/s as the ceil rate limit.

li.p: Apply the Stochastic Fairness Queuing (sfq) of classless qdisc to interface with a time interval of 60 seconds to reduce queue algorithm perturbation:
li.codeblock: # tc qdisc add dev ifb4eth0 parent 1:100 sfq perturb 60

li.p: Add the firewall mark (fw) filter to the interface:
li.codeblock: # tc filter add dev ifb4eth0 parent 1:0 protocol ip prio 100 handle 100 fw classid 1:100

li.p: Restore the packet meta mark from the connection mark (CONNMARK):
li.codeblock: # nft add rule ip mangle PREROUTING counter meta mark set ct mark
li.p: In this command, the nft utility has a mangle table with the PREROUTING chain rule specification that alters incoming packets before routing to replace the packet mark with CONNMARK.
If no nft table and chain exist, create a table and add a chain rule:
li.codeblock: # nft add table ip mangle # nft add chain ip mangle PREROUTING {type filter hook prerouting priority mangle \;}

li.p: Set the meta mark on tcp packets that are received on the specified destination address 192.0.2.3:
li.codeblock: # nft add rule ip mangle PREROUTING ip daddr 192.0.2.3 counter meta mark set 0x64

li.p: Save the packet mark into the connection mark:
li.codeblock: # nft add rule ip mangle PREROUTING counter ct mark set mark

li.p: Run the iperf3 utility as the server on a system by using the -s parameter and the server then waits for the response of the client connection:

step.cmd: On the client, run iperf3 as a client and connect to the server that listens on IP address 192.0.2.3 for periodic HTTP request-response timestamp:

info.p: 192.0.2.3 is the IP address of the server while 192.0.2.4 is the IP address of the client.

step.cmd: Terminate the iperf3 utility on the server by pressing Ctrl C :

info.codeblock:
Accepted connection from 192.0.2.4, port 52128 [5] local 192.0.2.3 port 5201 connected to 192.0.2.4 port 52130 [ID] Interval Transfer Bitrate [5] 0.00-1.00 sec 119 KBytes 973 Kbits/sec [5] 1.00-2.00 sec 116 KBytes 950 Kbits/sec ... [ID] Interval Transfer Bitrate [5] 0.00-14.81 sec 1.51 MBytes 853 Kbits/sec receiver
iperf3: interrupt - the server has terminated

step.cmd: Terminate the iperf3 utility on the client by pressing Ctrl C :

info.codeblock:
Connecting to host 192.0.2.3, port 5201 [5] local 192.0.2.4 port 52130 connected to 192.0.2.3 port 5201 [ID] Interval Transfer Bitrate Retr Cwnd [5] 0.00-1.00 sec 481 KBytes 3.94 Mbits/sec 0 76.4 KBytes [5] 1.00-2.00 sec 223 KBytes 1.83 Mbits/sec 0 82.0 KBytes ... [ID] Interval Transfer Bitrate Retr [5] 0.00-14.00 sec 3.92 MBytes 2.35 Mbits/sec 32 sender [5] 0.00-14.00 sec 0.00 Bytes 0.00 bits/sec receiver
iperf3: error - the server has terminated

li.p: Display the statistics about packet counts of the htb and sfq classes on the interface:
li.codeblock:
# tc -s qdisc show dev ifb4eth0
qdisc htb 1: root ... Sent 26611455 bytes 3054 pkt (dropped 76, overlimits 4887 requeues 0) ... qdisc sfq 8001: parent ... Sent 26535030 bytes 2296 pkt (dropped 76, overlimits 0 requeues 0) ...

li.p: Display the statistics of packet counts for the mirred and ctinfo actions:
li.codeblock:
# tc -s filter show dev enp1s0 ingress filter parent ffff: protocol ip pref 49152 u32 chain 0 filter parent ffff: protocol ip pref 49152 u32 chain 0 fh 800: ht divisor 1 filter parent ffff: protocol ip pref 49152 u32 chain 0 fh 800::800 order 2048 key ht 800 bkt 0 terminal flowid not_in_hw (rule hit 8075 success 8075) match 00000000/00000000 at 0 (success 8075 ) action order 1: ctinfo zone 0 pipe index 1 ref 1 bind 1 cpmark 0x00000064 installed 3105 sec firstused 3105 sec DSCP set 0 error 0 CPMARK set 7712 Action statistics: Sent 25891504 bytes 3137 pkt (dropped 0, overlimits 0 requeues 0) backlog 0b 0p requeues 0
action order 2: mirred (Egress Redirect to device ifb4eth0) stolen index 1 ref 1 bind 1 installed 3105 sec firstused 3105 sec Action statistics: Sent 25891504 bytes 3137 pkt (dropped 0, overlimits 61 requeues 0) backlog 0b 0p requeues 0

li.p: Display the statistics of the htb rate-limiter and its configuration:
li.codeblock: # tc -s class show dev ifb4eth0 class htb 1:100 root leaf 8001: prio 7 rate 1Mbit ceil 2Mbit burst 1600b cburst 1600b Sent 26541716 bytes 2373 pkt (dropped 61, overlimits 4887 requeues 0) backlog 0b 0p requeues 0 lended: 7248 borrowed: 0 giants: 0 tokens: 187250 ctokens: 93625
